description: A React Native app that helps non-native English speakers improve rhythm, stress, linking, and prosody through daily exercises, AI feedback, and real-world speaking scenarios.

userStories.title: Define curriculum JSON schema with Pydantic models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a validated schema for curriculum content so exercises load consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/schema/ directory with Pydantic models for Day, Exercise, and MeetingPhrase
Exercise types enum: 'stress' | 'linking' | 'chunk' | 'shadow' | 'intonation'
Day structure: { day: int, theme: str, exercises: list[Exercise] }
Exercise structure: { id: str, type: ExerciseType, title: str, instruction: str, targetText: str, stressPattern: optional list, chunks: optional list, audioUrl: optional str, tips: list[str] }
MeetingPhrase structure: { id: str, category: str, text: str, audioUrl: optional str }
JSON Schema file exported to content/schema/curriculum.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Day 1 curriculum content — Word Stress Basics
userStories.description: As a user, I want Day 1 exercises on word stress so I can start learning immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/curriculum/day-01.json with theme 'Word Stress Basics'
Contains 4 exercises: 2 stress drills, 1 chunk speaking, 1 intonation
Each exercise has targetText, stressPattern (array of booleans for stressed syllables), instruction, and tips
Example sentences use common professional vocabulary
JSON validates against the schema from US-001
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Day 2 curriculum content — Linking Words Together
userStories.description: As a user, I want Day 2 exercises on linking so I stop speaking word-by-word.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/curriculum/day-02.json with theme 'Linking Words Together'
Contains 4 exercises: 2 linking practice, 1 chunk speaking, 1 stress drill
Linking exercises show connected forms (e.g., 'pick_it_up')
JSON validates against schema
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Day 3 curriculum content — Speaking in Chunks
userStories.description: As a user, I want Day 3 exercises on thought groups so my speech flows naturally.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/curriculum/day-03.json with theme 'Speaking in Chunks'
Contains 4 exercises: 2 chunk speaking, 1 shadowing, 1 linking
Chunk exercises have 'chunks' array dividing sentences into thought groups
JSON validates against schema
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create meeting phrases JSON content
userStories.description: As a user, I want common meeting phrases so I can practice real work situations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/phrases/meetings.json
4 categories: 'updates', 'opinions', 'clarifications', 'presenting'
Each category has 5-8 phrases with text and empty audioUrl placeholder
JSON validates against schema
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: FastAPI project scaffolding with health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend project structure so other APIs can be built on top.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/ directory with FastAPI app in backend/app/main.py
GET /health returns { status: 'ok' }
CORS middleware configured for React Native requests
Requirements file: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, pydub, python-multipart
Dockerfile for containerized deployment
App runs with 'uvicorn backend.app.main:app'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User progress database schema and models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database models to store user progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/app/models.py with SQLAlchemy models
User model: id, native_language, english_level, goal, created_at
SessionResult model: id, user_id, day, exercises_completed, rhythm_score, stress_score, pacing_score, intonation_score, completed_at
SQLite database created at backend/data/app.db
Database initialization on app startup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User progress REST API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need endpoints to store and retrieve user progress.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/users creates user profile, returns user_id
POST /api/v1/progress saves session result (day, scores, exercises completed)
GET /api/v1/progress/{user_id} returns full progress history
GET /api/v1/progress/{user_id}/summary returns aggregated stats: streak, averages, total_sessions, trend
Streak resets when a day is skipped (no difficulty adjustment)
Tests pass with sample data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Speech analyzer abstract interface and free analyzer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the speech analysis abstraction and a free implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/app/analyzers/base.py with abstract SpeechAnalyzer class
Analysis result dataclass: rhythm_score (1-5), stress_score (1-5), pacing_score (1-5), intonation_score (1-5), feedback_items (list of {type, message})
Create backend/app/analyzers/free.py implementing FreeAnalyzer
FreeAnalyzer uses librosa for tempo/rhythm analysis and parselmouth for pitch/intonation
Accepts WAV/M4A audio + target text, returns analysis result
Feedback types: 'good', 'warning', 'tip' with actionable messages
Add librosa, parselmouth, numpy to requirements
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: BYOP analyzer implementations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need provider-specific analyzers for users who bring their own API keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/app/analyzers/azure.py implementing AzureAnalyzer using Azure Speech Services
Create backend/app/analyzers/google.py implementing GoogleAnalyzer using Google Cloud Speech
Create backend/app/analyzers/openai.py implementing OpenAIAnalyzer using OpenAI Whisper API
Each accepts API key as constructor parameter
All return the same unified AnalysisResult format from base.py
Factory function get_analyzer(provider, api_key) returns correct implementation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audio analysis REST API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the endpoint that accepts audio recordings and returns feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/analyze accepts multipart form: audio file (WAV/M4A) + target_text + provider (optional) + api_key (optional)
Converts audio to WAV using pydub if needed
Uses FreeAnalyzer by default, or BYOP analyzer if provider and api_key provided
Returns JSON: { rhythm_score, stress_score, pacing_score, intonation_score, feedback: [{type, message}] }
Response time < 5 seconds for 10-second audio clip
Returns 400 for invalid audio format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expo project setup with bottom tab navigation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the React Native app structure with navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create mobile/ directory with Expo managed workflow (npx create-expo-app)
Bottom tab navigation with 4 tabs: Home, Dashboard, Library, Settings
Each tab shows a placeholder screen with the tab name
Tab icons using @expo/vector-icons
TypeScript configured
App builds and runs without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Onboarding profile setup screen
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to set up my profile so the app can tailor exercises to my level.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding screen shown on first launch (check AsyncStorage for profile)
Select native language (Vietnamese pre-selected)
Select English level: Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced
Select goal: Meetings / Presentations / General confidence
Profile saved to AsyncStorage and POST to backend /api/v1/users
After completing, navigates to Home tab
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Daily lesson home screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see today's lesson when I open the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen shows current day (e.g., 'Day 3 of 14')
Displays lesson theme, estimated time (~10 min), and exercise list
Each exercise shows title, type icon, and completion status
Practice streak counter displayed at top
Curriculum data loaded from bundled JSON files
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Audio playback component for example sentences
userStories.description: As a user, I want to hear example audio so I know the target rhythm.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable AudioPlayer component using expo-av
Play/pause button with visual state indicator
Displays sentence text with stressed syllables in bold
Chunk boundaries shown with slashes or spacing
Accepts audioUrl and sentence metadata as props
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Audio recording component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to record myself speaking and play it back.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable AudioRecorder component using expo-av
Tap-to-record button with red pulsing indicator while recording
Auto-stops after 10 seconds of silence or manual stop
Playback button to hear own recording
Re-record option before submitting
Requests microphone permission with clear explanation text
Returns recorded audio URI to parent component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Feedback display component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see clear feedback on my recording.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable FeedbackCard component
Shows scores as 1-5 dots for: rhythm, stress, pacing, intonation
Displays feedback items with indicators: ✅ good (green), ⚠️ warning (yellow), 💡 tip (blue)
Loading state while analysis runs
Accepts analysis result as props
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Stress drill exercise screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to practice word and sentence stress.
acceptanceCriteria: Exercise screen for type='stress'
Shows instruction text at top
Displays sentence with stressed syllables highlighted (bold + color)
Integrates AudioPlayer for example, AudioRecorder for user, FeedbackCard for results
Flow: listen example → record → get feedback (calls /api/v1/analyze)
'Next exercise' button after viewing feedback
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Linking practice exercise screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to practice linking words together.
acceptanceCriteria: Exercise screen for type='linking'
Shows connected speech with underscores or ties between linked words
Visual indicators showing where words link (e.g., 'pick‿it‿up')
Same listen → record → feedback flow as stress drills
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Chunk speaking exercise screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to practice speaking in thought groups.
acceptanceCriteria: Exercise screen for type='chunk'
Sentence displayed divided into chunks with visual separators (slashes or cards)
Example audio demonstrates natural pauses between chunks
Same listen → record → feedback flow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Intonation training exercise screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to practice rising/falling intonation patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Exercise screen for type='intonation'
Shows sentence with intonation arrows: ↗ rising, ↘ falling
Covers question vs statement patterns
Same listen → record → feedback flow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Shadowing mode exercise screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to speak along with model audio and compare rhythm.
acceptanceCriteria: Exercise screen for type='shadow'
Play button starts model audio while simultaneously recording user
After completion, shows simplified rhythm dots comparing model vs user timing
Side-by-side playback: model audio then user recording
Submits to /api/v1/analyze for feedback
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Exercise router — render correct screen by exercise type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a router that renders the right exercise screen based on type.
acceptanceCriteria: ExerciseScreen component accepts exercise data and renders correct type screen
Routes: stress → StressDrill, linking → LinkingPractice, chunk → ChunkSpeaking, shadow → ShadowingMode, intonation → IntonationTraining
Tapping exercise on Home screen navigates to ExerciseScreen with exercise data
Back button returns to Home screen
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Session completion screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want a satisfying completion screen after finishing a daily session.
acceptanceCriteria: Shown after completing all exercises for the day
Displays: today's average score, streak count, improvement vs yesterday
Rotated motivational message from a preset list
'Done for today' button returns to Home
Saves session result to backend via POST /api/v1/progress
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rhythm score dashboard screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my progress over time so I stay motivated.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard tab screen fetches data from GET /api/v1/progress/{user_id}/summary
Line chart showing overall rhythm score over days (react-native-chart-kit or victory-native)
Stats cards: practice streak, total sessions, average score
Breakdown by category: rhythm, stress, pacing, intonation
Daily/weekly toggle for chart view
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Meeting phrase library screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse and practice common meeting phrases.
acceptanceCriteria: Library tab screen loads data from bundled meetings.json
4 category sections: Updates, Opinions, Clarifications, Presenting
Each phrase shows text, tap to expand with practice option
Tapping 'Practice' opens exercise flow (listen → record → feedback)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 14-day program overview screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the full 14-day program and my progress through it.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible from Home screen via 'View Program' link
Shows all 14 days with day number, theme, and completion status
Completed days marked with checkmark
Days unlock sequentially (tapping locked day shows confirmation to skip)
Tapping unlocked day navigates to that day's lesson
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Settings screen with BYOP API key configuration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure my own speech API key for better feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings tab screen with 'Speech API' section
Provider dropdown: Free (default) / Azure / Google / OpenAI
API key text input (shown only when non-free provider selected)
API key stored in expo-secure-store (encrypted)
Test button that calls /api/v1/analyze with a sample to verify the key works
Success/error feedback after test
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create remaining curriculum content — Days 4-14
userStories.description: As a user, I want the full 14-day program of exercises.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/curriculum/day-04.json through day-14.json
Day 4-5: Shadowing exercises introduced
Day 6-7: Meeting phrases and professional scenarios
Day 8-10: Mixed drills combining all exercise types
Day 11-13: Advanced linking, reduction, and natural speech patterns
Day 14: Final review with all exercise types
Each day has 3-5 exercises, all validate against schema
Typecheck passes
